items.name: Academy Mentor
items.description: The game's companion skill: turns the real Claude into a mentor for "AI Expedition." It checks your real missions against a checklist, gives you tougher assignments, and re-explains topics with a quiz.
items.useFor: Every Academy explorer. Copy a mission with the "Copy mission for the mentor" button, paste it into a chat with Claude — and get an honest verdict: ✅ MISSION PASSED or 🔄 NEEDS WORK.
items.install: See companion/README.md in the game's repo: paste SKILL.md into your skill on claude.ai or into your project's .claude/skills/

items.name: docx
items.description: Creating, reading, and editing Word documents (.docx): styles, tables of contents, headers and footers, tables, tracked changes.
items.useFor: Anyone who prepares reports, letters, and memos. For example: "Put together a quarterly report in Word with a table of contents and a company letterhead."

items.name: pdf
items.description: Working with PDFs: extracting text and tables, merging and splitting files, filling forms, watermarks, OCR of scans.
items.useFor: Lawyers, accountants, managers. For example: "Merge these three PDFs into one and add page numbers" or "Pull the table out of this scanned invoice."

items.name: pptx
items.description: Creating and editing PowerPoint presentations (.pptx): slides, layouts, speaker notes, templates.
items.useFor: Anyone who gives presentations. For example: "Make a 10-slide pitch deck from this business plan in our brand colors."

items.name: xlsx
items.description: Working with Excel spreadsheets (.xlsx, .csv): formulas, formatting, charts, cleaning up messy data, converting formats.
items.useFor: Analysts and finance folks. For example: "Clean up this sales CSV, add a pivot table and a chart by month."

items.name: doc-coauthoring
items.description: A structured process for co-authoring documentation: transferring context, iterative refinement, and testing the text on readers.
items.useFor: Tech writers and team leads. For example: "Help me write a technical spec for the new API — walk me through it from draft to final."

items.name: internal-comms
items.description: Writing internal communications in your company's formats: status reports, leadership updates, newsletters, FAQs, incident reports.
items.useFor: Managers and leaders. For example: "Turn these meeting notes into a weekly status report for the director."

items.name: brand-guidelines
items.description: Applies Anthropic's brand colors and typography to any artifact. Serves as a model for how to build a skill around your own brand guidelines.
items.useFor: Designers and marketers, as a template: copy the structure and drop in your company's colors so Claude always styles materials in your look.

items.name: canvas-design
items.description: Creating visual designs — posters, covers, artwork — in PNG and PDF with a thought-out design philosophy.
items.useFor: Anyone who needs static graphics. For example: "Make a poster for the internal conference in a minimalist style."

items.name: theme-factory
items.description: A set of 10 ready-made visual themes (colors + fonts) for styling slides, documents, reports, and landing pages, plus generating new themes on the fly.
items.useFor: Anyone who wants a consistent style without a designer. For example: "Style this report in the 'ocean' theme" or "Come up with a theme to match our logo."

items.name: algorithmic-art
items.description: Generative art with p5.js: flow fields, particle systems, reproducible randomness with interactive parameters.
items.useFor: Creatives and developers. For example: "Generate an abstract background of flow lines in our brand colors."

items.name: slack-gif-creator
items.description: Creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack's constraints (size, frame rate), with validation tools.
items.useFor: Teams on Slack. For example: "Make a GIF of a dancing robot for the release celebration channel."

items.name: frontend-design
items.description: Building expressive, production-grade web interfaces: pages, dashboards, React components with high design quality.
items.useFor: Frontend developers. For example: "Build a product landing page that doesn't look like a stock template."

items.name: web-artifacts-builder
items.description: Building complex, multi-component HTML artifacts for claude.ai with React, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui — with state and routing.
items.useFor: People building interactive artifacts in Claude. For example: "Build an interactive mortgage calculator with tabs and charts."

items.name: webapp-testing
items.description: Testing web apps with Playwright: scripts open pages, click, fill in forms, and check the result.
items.useFor: QA and developers. For example: "Check that the login form on localhost works, and take a screenshot of every step."

items.name: mcp-builder
items.description: A guide to building high-quality MCP servers in Python (FastMCP) or TypeScript (MCP SDK) for integrating external APIs.
items.useFor: Integration developers. For example: "Build an MCP server for our internal orders API so Claude can search for orders."

items.name: claude-api
items.description: An up-to-date reference for the Claude API and Anthropic SDK: model IDs, pricing, parameters, streaming, tool use, caching.
items.useFor: Developers building on the Claude API. For example: "Write a client with prompt caching and token counting — pull the current model IDs from the skill, not from memory."

items.name: skill-creator
items.description: A skill for creating skills: helps you write a new skill from scratch, improve an existing one, and measure its effectiveness with evals.
items.useFor: Anyone who wants to package up their workflows. For example: "Create a skill that formats our release notes using an internal template."

items.name: supabase/agent-skills
items.description: Official Supabase skills: schema development, migrations, Postgres best practices, and security. Compatible with 18+ AI agents.
items.useFor: Supabase/Postgres developers. For example: "Design a database schema with RLS policies following Supabase best practices."

items.name: vercel-labs/agent-skills
items.description: Skills from Vercel: react-best-practices (40+ React/Next.js performance rules from Vercel's engineers) and vercel-optimize (an audit of a project's cost and speed).
items.useFor: React/Next.js developers. For example: "Check my components against Vercel's performance rules and find unnecessary re-renders."

items.name: expo/skills
items.description: Official skills from the Expo team for building, deploying, updating, and debugging mobile apps with Expo and React Native.
items.useFor: Mobile developers. For example: "Update the app to the new Expo SDK version and set up an EAS build for iOS and Android."

items.name: heygen-com/hyperframes
items.description: HeyGen HyperFrames skills: programmable HTML video — composing scenes, linting, preview and render, plus TTS and transcription.
items.useFor: People making videos with code. For example: "Build a 15-second promo clip with animated text and a voiceover."

items.name: superpowers (obra)
items.description: A popular development methodology for agents: spec and plan first, then TDD and subagent-driven development. The skills trigger automatically.
items.useFor: Developers who need discipline from the agent. For example: "Build feature X" — the agent derives the spec and plan itself and works for hours following TDD.

items.name: vercel-labs/skills (find-skills)
items.description: Vercel's skills.sh CLI ecosystem and the find-skills meta-skill: search for and install skills from an open catalog with a single command.
items.useFor: Anyone who wants to discover new skills. For example: "Find a skill for working with Stripe" — the agent searches the skills.sh catalog itself and offers to install it.
